worktree_preflight_blockers: ckc-overhaul-launch: unknown noncanonical artifact directory under ../Handshake_Artifacts
managed_pgdata: unknown noncanonical artifact directory under ../Handshake_Artifacts
source_control.worktree_preflight_note: The branch and worktree exist and are clean, but just worktree-add exited nonzero after creation because artifact hygiene policy preserves unknown noncanonical artifact directories until operator review.

scope.summary: Define and implement modern Handshake local/cloud/subagent swarm lane launch and communication so models speak through typed Handshake state rather than hidden chat or peer channels.
scope.why: The original v02.122 multi-model spec predated current subagent and cloud/local swarm practice. Handshake now needs explicit ModelLaneRun, ModelLane, ModelLaneMessage, ContextBundle, artifact promotion, consent, diagnostics, and recovery contracts.
forbidden_paths: wholesale spec restructuring outside the owning LLM Infrastructure module
direct cloud calls outside LlmClient/CloudLane boundaries
npm/JavaScript proof targets for this WP
acceptance_criteria: Local model lanes launch through LlmClient -> ModelRuntime and produce ModelLane state.
Cloud model lanes launch through CloudLane/BYOK with ProjectionPlan and ConsentReceipt where required.
Subagents are modeled as ModelLane records with stable lane ids, session ids, status, and recovery metadata.
All cloud/local/subagent exchange is represented as coordinator-mediated ModelLaneMessage plus payload artifacts.
A downstream model receives prior model output through ContextBundle or artifact refs, not hidden conversational memory.
Lane outputs remain advisory until promoted into code, WP/MT contracts, or validator verdicts.
Diagnostics expose ModelLaneRun, lane status, messages, artifact refs, and recoverability failures.
Validation covers local-only, cloud-only, mixed cloud/local, subagent, disagreement, consent-block, orphan, restart, and missing-payload cases.
Durable model-lane state uses Rust backend code and PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore authority; SQLite is not allowed as runtime, cache, fixture, fallback, or test authority.
Collaborative/operator-model state uses CRDT draft semantics where live shared edits are possible, then promotes through ValidationRunner/PromotionGate before becoming authority.
Every observable lane behavior records Flight Recorder/EventLedger evidence plus the HBR-INT-009 three-tier diagnostic outcome for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, and Palmistry.

The project-named internal viewing tool renders lane topology, message flow, artifacts, validation, promotion, runtime errors, and recovery hints for visual/debug verification; naming is owned by the separate viewing-tool worktree/session.

The in-product internal UserManual reflects every ModelLane/cloud/local/subagent orchestration behavior introduced by this WP, including usage, recovery, verification, Flight Recorder/EventLedger linkage, and HBR-INT-009 Flight Recorder/internal_diagnostics/Palmistry posture.

All product implementation and proof for this WP is Rust-only: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus surfaces. Legacy TypeScript/React/app/src surfaces are reference-only and must not become implementation or proof authority.

The ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage contract explicitly carries trace/span, runtime, capability, tool, payload, replay, recovery, promotion, CRDT, and Locus ownership fields: trace_id, run_span_id, lane_span_id, message_span_id, parent_span_id or linked_span_contexts, adapter_id, runtime_binding, capability_token_ids or effective_capability_snapshot_ref, projection_plan_ref, consent_receipt_ref, tool_gate_decision_refs, idempotency_key, payload_sha256, replay_order_key, event_ledger_stream_id, event_ledger_append_id or event_ledger_event_id, event_ledger_seq, event_stream_version, transaction_seq, replay_after_event_ledger_seq, promoted_artifact_ref, promoted_artifact_sha256, promoted_artifact_version, promotion_gate_ref, promotion_receipt_ref, validator_verdict_ref, operator_decision_ref, crdt_update_ref, crdt_base_snapshot_ref, crdt_state_vector, crdt_proposal_ref, crdt_stale_base_ref, recovery_state, heartbeat_at_utc, lease_expires_at_utc, reclaim_after_utc, restart_generation, cancellation_ref, backpressure_ref, loop_counter_ref, failstate_code, reason_ref, recovery_hint_ref, work_packet_id, micro_task_id, task_board_id, coordinator_session_id, owner_session, and locus_binding_ref.

MT lifecycle/status signaling is restartable: every MT contract carries claimed_by, claimed_at_utc, ready_for_validation_by, ready_for_validation_at_utc, completed_by, blocker, missing_resource, proof_status, hbr_status, kb_ready_checklist_receipt_id, product_worktree_root_resolution, last_recovery_event_ref, and last_runtime_status_ref.

Proof commands for implementation MTs must use exact Rust test targets or exact test names and must fail on zero matching tests. Provider calls may use deterministic fakes; durable authority must touch PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT/UserManual/Argus Rust resources.

Dexterity is the internal model switching and launch kernel name for this WP. It is a kernel/manual/display name only; stable schema IDs remain ModelLaneRun, ModelLane, ModelLaneMessage, and hsk.model_lane_*@1.
The provisional Rust-native diagnostics/navigation contract id is native_swarm_lane_diagnostics until the separate naming worktree supplies an approved alias.

Each MT receives post-implementation adversarial review through runtime launch, PostgreSQL/EventLedger/CRDT recovery, Rust native Argus/UserManual, and Spec-Realism/status-signaling lenses before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

architecture_directives.backend_language: Rust-only product implementation for this WP. Runtime, state, adapters, storage, diagnostics, UserManual APIs, and proof harnesses must be Rust backend or Rust native egui/Argus code.
architecture_directives.frontend_language: If GUI/debug/operator surface work is required, implement it in src/frontend/handshake_native Rust native surfaces with Argus-compatible AccessKit/egui evidence. Do not add TypeScript, React, Tauri, WebView, or npm proof requirements.
architecture_directives.durable_storage: PostgreSQL plus EventLedger, ArtifactStore, ContextBundle, TraceProjection, and Flight Recorder are canonical. Markdown, UI rows, debug reports, HTML, and chat text are projections only.
architecture_directives.collaboration_model: CRDT draft/update semantics are required for any shared editable surface touched by operator and model lanes. Authority changes require ValidationRunner or PromotionGate evidence.
architecture_directives.visual_debug_surface_contract: This WP must consume the project-approved internal viewing-tool name from the separate viewing-tool worktree/session. The surface must inspect lane runs, messages, artifacts, tool calls, validation, promotion, runtime errors, and recovery state.
architecture_directives.internal_user_manual_contract: Every implementation MT in this WP must update the in-product internal UserManual for the behavior it changes. Manual entries must explain no-context usage and connect the behavior to Flight Recorder/EventLedger plus HBR-INT-009 Flight Recorder/internal_diagnostics/Palmistry posture; Palmistry/internal_diagnostics absence is DEFERRED-with-reason, never silently skipped.
architecture_directives.product_reference_posture: HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md is reference/navigation only. Implementation authority resolves through SPEC_CURRENT, master-spec-v02.197, build rules, and product code evidence.
architecture_directives.legacy_frontend_posture: app/src legacy React/Tauri/TypeScript surfaces are reference-only for this WP; implementation and proof authority is Rust backend plus Rust native frontend/debug surfaces.
architecture_directives.model_exchange_pillar_translation: Dexterity launches and switches model lanes; models propose/critique/plan through typed ModelLaneMessage/ContextBundle/artifact refs; Handshake validates/promotes deterministically; PostgreSQL/EventLedger holds authority; Flight Recorder/internal_diagnostics/Palmistry record observability posture; Loom/Locus/FEMS/UserManual make the work navigable and recoverable.
architecture_directives.rust_only_hard_gate: No new TypeScript, React, app/src, app/src-tauri, Tauri/WebView, JavaScript, npm, or docs/model-manual proof may satisfy this WP. Those surfaces are reference-only unless future authority explicitly reactivates them outside this WP.
architecture_directives.status_recovery_contract: MT status signaling must be restartable and validator-honest: implementers may set CLAIMED/READY_FOR_VALIDATION plus proof/blocker fields; completed_by remains validator-only.
architecture_directives.post_implementation_review_contract: Every MT gets pre-implementation contract review and post-implementation adversarial review against the actual diff before READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Review findings must be addressed in product code/contracts or represented as explicit MT lifecycle blocker/missing_resource/proof_status state. Implementers do not mark COMPLETED.

hbr.requirement: Typed EventLedger append plus replay reconstructs ModelLaneRun, ModelLane, and ModelLaneMessage behavior.
Model invocation dispatches through stored ContextBundle, ModelRuntime/CloudLane adapter, ToolGate for tool calls, and ArtifactStore output; no invocation bypass.
Lane output reaches authority only through PromotionGate or explicit validator/operator promotion; direct mutation fails closed.
CRDT draft updates persist, replay after reconnect, and promote through ValidationRunner before becoming authority when shared editable state is touched.
Every local, CLI bridge, BYOK/cloud, manual, and subagent lane normalizes into the same SessionRun/ModelLane event stream.
The project-named internal viewing tool reconstructs the behavior from durable state alone: no chat history, terminal scrollback, or provider memory.
Every observable runtime behavior records the three-tier diagnostic outcome: Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, and Palmistry as WIRED, NOT_APPLICABLE-with-reason, or DEFERRED-with-reason.
At least two concurrent agent/model lanes plus simulated operator edits run against touched shared state without silent overwrite, deadlock, or starvation.
Leases, locks, queues, cancellation, backpressure, and loop counters are bounded and recover stale lane sessions.
Every cross-agent/model message uses typed routing fields and can be acted on without prose parsing.
Drive the running operator surface or project-named internal viewing-tool equivalent, capture it, and inspect the rendered result.
For non-UI behavior, the project-named internal viewing tool must answer task, context, return, tool calls, artifact, validation, and promotion from durable state.
Automated model-lane tests run headless or hidden without focus/Z-order changes.
Background model, CLI, subagent, or sandbox launches record ownership metadata and reclaim on close, failure, staleness, or cancel.
Any model-callable command/tool/IPC/config/operator capability updates the in-product ModelManual/UserManual in the same change.
Manual content lets a no-context model operate the feature: purpose, workflows, startup, commands, expected I/O, navigation, constraints, failures, and recovery.
Manual entries are code-truthful and self-consistency tests verify named surfaces exist.

Every implementation that changes a Handshake behavior, tool, feature, primitive, workflow, model lane, diagnostic surface, storage/event contract, or operator/model navigation path updates the internal UserManual in the same change with Flight Recorder/EventLedger linkage and Palmistry/internal_diagnostics posture.

research_basis.reason: Operator requested research-backed MT strengthening before implementation. Sources were used as evidence patterns only, then translated through current master spec and product code authority.

external_sources_checked.name: Hugging Face Candle Rust ML runtime

relevant_patterns_found: Current multi-agent systems emphasize stateful orchestration, tool use, human-in-the-loop oversight, debugging/evaluation, and long-running workflows.

Field CRDT implementations expose explicit updates, state vectors, snapshots, sync protocols, and rich-text intent issues; Handshake must not treat merged state as automatically semantically accepted.
Distributed tracing standards propagate trace/span context across process boundaries and use trace data as an operations/debug substrate.
Durable workflow systems separate deterministic workflow replay from non-deterministic external activities.
PostgreSQL event-sourcing implementations use append-only event streams, optimistic concurrency, snapshots, projections, ACK/NACK, and concurrent consumers.

Local model runtimes commonly expose OpenAI-compatible endpoints, while Rust-native ML runtimes exist; Handshake must normalize both behind ModelRuntime rather than letting endpoint shape define authority.
Postgres-backed vector search supports keeping retrieval data with application data, aligning Loom/FEMS/UserManual retrieval with PostgreSQL authority.
reuse_opportunities: Reuse existing Rust ModelRuntime/CloudLane/SwarmCoordinator/SessionBroker/ContextBundle/CRDT/Flight Recorder/Loom/Locus/UserManual modules before adding new primitives.
Reuse existing UserManual live PostgreSQL/EventLedger tests as the proof model for MT-010/MT-011.
Reuse existing kernel CRDT stale-base, denial, snapshot, and state-vector tests for MT-005/MT-007/MT-009 proof design.
Reuse existing Flight Recorder span/event repositories for lane trace correlation instead of introducing a second trace store.
rejected_options: peer-to-peer model channels outside coordinator-owned ModelLaneMessage records
legacy app/src React/Tauri code as runtime authority
SQLite runtime/test/fixture/cache/fallback authority for this WP
Flight Recorder-only replay without PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rows
manual or markdown prose as product runtime authority
research_basis.selected_approach: Keep WP-1 as a Rust/PostgreSQL/EventLedger-first ModelLane orchestration packet: typed lane schemas, deterministic routing/promotion, ContextBundle/Loom/FEMS handoff, cloud consent/projection, recovery/replay/diagnostics, validation harnesses, and UserManual currency.
risks: MTs could still be implemented through old app/src UI or endpoint-specific shortcuts unless contracts explicitly forbid reference-only authority leaks.
CRDT mechanical convergence could be mistaken for semantic acceptance of model edits.
Flight Recorder could be overloaded as replay authority despite master spec requiring PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority.
Manual updates could become markdown-only and fail no-context product operation.
Cloud/local adapters could share request shapes but diverge in consent, capability, trace, or recovery behavior.
mitigations: Each MT now states native Rust/backend authority and legacy app/src reference-only posture where relevant.
CRDT-touched MTs require proposal/draft/state-vector/stale-base/promotion metadata and semantic review before authority.
Recovery MTs require replay from PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT records without Flight Recorder/provider trace history.
MT-010/MT-011 require canonical UserManual rows/API/navigation with self-consistency and freshness proof.
MT-003/MT-006 require capability/consent/Flight Recorder/EventLedger/result-capture boundary for all tool/provider paths.

assumptions_to_attack: The existing swarm runtime already models enough state for model-to-model communication.
Cloud and local model outputs can be passed through prompts without durable artifacts.
Subagents can be treated as ordinary chat participants without lane identity.
Diagnostics can be added after implementation rather than as a contract requirement.
minimum_controls: No peer-to-peer hidden channels: every exchange must become typed lane message plus artifact ref.
No cloud bypass: CloudLane/BYOK consent/projection checks remain mandatory.
No advisory promotion confusion: only explicit promotion changes authority.
No orphan subagents: lane status and recovery metadata must be canonical.
